title: Actors, entertainers and presenters
description: Teach or instruct individuals or groups for the primary purpose of self-enrichment or recreation, rather than for an occupational objective, educational attainment, competition, or fitness. Formulate design concepts and presentation approaches for visual productions and media, such as print, broadcasting, video, and film. Direct workers engaged in artwork or layout design. All artists and related workers not listed separately. (Based on 21 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Pose for artists and photographers.
Announce musical selections, station breaks, commercials, or public service information, and accept requests from the listening audience.
Sing or dance during dramatic or comedic performances.
Work with other crew members responsible for lighting, costumes, make-up, and props.
Portray and interpret roles using speech, gestures, and body movements to entertain, inform, or instruct audiences in radio, film, television, or live settings.
Attend auditions and casting calls to audition for roles.
Perform humorous and serious interpretations of emotions, actions, and situations, using body movements, facial expressions, and gestures.
Collaborate with other actors as part of an ensemble.
Learn about characters in scripts and their relationships to develop role interpretations, study and rehearse their lines, stunts, and cues as directed.
Work closely with directors, other actors, and playwrights to find the interpretation most suited to the role.
Moderate panels or discussion shows on topics such as current affairs, art, or education, and provide commentary and conduct interviews during various events.
Attend press conferences to gather information for broadcast and report on various subjects, ensuring accurate and engaging delivery.
Coordinate games, contests, or other on-air competitions, performing such duties as asking questions and awarding prizes.
Locate and interview guests for talk or interview shows, providing insights on their lives and work or current topics of interest.
Record commercials for broadcast and produce commentary for news segments, ensuring quality and adherence to the intended message.
Promote productions through interviews and by making promotional appearances at various events.
Write and edit scripts, develop storylines for broadcasts, and create narratives by researching background information and interviewing relevant subjects.
Conduct taped or filmed interviews or narratives, and communicate findings to the public through various media formats.
Analyze and interpret news and current events, researching and checking reference materials to provide accurate and comprehensive coverage.
Interpret and reconstruct performances and pieces by working with musicians and collaborating on musical arrangements.
Perform before live audiences in various settings such as concerts, theatrical performances, and social gatherings.
Handle administrative tasks related to performances and productions, including budgeting and contract negotiations.
Establish and maintain relationships with credible sources and relevant individuals in various fields to enhance production quality and integrity.
Direct live broadcasts, films, recordings, or non-broadcast programming for public entertainment or education, coordinating various crew and production personnel throughout the process.
Study and research scripts to determine how they should be directed, and become familiar with production concepts and requirements.
Direct shows, productions, and plays, and coordinate activities of personnel engaged in broadcast news, sports, or programming.

Confer with technical directors, managers, crew members, and writers to discuss details of production, such as photography, script, music, sets, and costumes; and conduct meetings with staff to discuss production progress and ensure production objectives are attained.
Research production topics using various informational sources and obtain rights to scripts or existing video footage.
Review films, recordings, or rehearsals to ensure conformance to production and broadcast standards.
Select plays, scripts, books, or ideas to be produced and review performer information to decide whom to audition for parts.
Hold auditions for parts, negotiate contracts with actors deemed suitable for specific roles, and hire principal cast and crew members.
Perform management activities, such as budgeting, scheduling, planning, and marketing, and monitor and review programming to ensure guidelines are adhered to.
Confer with writers, producers, or actors about script changes or run rehearsals with writers and actors to create final drafts.
Compile scripts, program notes, and other materials related to productions; create worksheets for dressing lists, show notes, or costume checks.
Develop ideas for programs and features that a station could produce, and propose marketing plans for finished products, collaborating with sales associates to supervise product distribution.
Edit films and videotapes to insert music, dialogue, and sound effects, and trim film segments to specified lengths to present stories with maximum effect.
Plan and schedule programming and event coverage based on various factors including community needs and viewer demographics.
Negotiate contracts with performers and maintain talent files that include information about performers' specialties, availability, and past performances.
Direct technical aspects of productions and monitor broadcasts to ensure programs conform to network policies and regulations.
Prepare students for development by encouraging them to explore learning opportunities and adapt teaching methods to meet varying needs and interests.
Instruct students individually and in groups, using various teaching methods, and monitor performance to ensure standards are satisfied.
Create custom illustrations or other graphic elements and compose and edit scripts.
Review and approve art materials and proofs of printed copy developed by staff members; select and combine effective shots to create a logical, smoothly running story.
Organize and string together raw footage into a continuous whole according to scripts; verify key numbers and time codes on materials.
Negotiate with various parties involved in productions, including independent producers and distributors.
Monitor, maintain, or secure inventories of costumes and wigs; purchase or requisition costumes and wardrobe necessities.
Review scripts for production information regarding locale, period, and required costumes; and check the appearance of costumes on stage to ensure desired effects.
Prepare materials and classrooms for class activities and clean and press costumes before performances.
Provide dressing assistance to cast members and arrange costumes in order of use for quick changes during performances.
Conduct interviews for broadcasts and prepare copy for promotional announcements.
Prepare and administer written, oral, and performance tests, and issue grades in accordance with performance.

Collaborate with others to prepare or perform artistic productions.
Coordinate logistics for productions or events.
Study scripts to determine project requirements.

Operate communications, transmissions, or broadcasting equipment.
Operate control consoles for sound, lighting or video.
Interview others for news or entertainment purposes.
Gather information about work conditions or locations.
Participate in physical training to maintain fitness.
Construct distinctive physical objects for artistic, functional, or commercial purposes.
Prepare financial documents, reports, or budgets.
Report information to managers or other personnel.
Manage content of broadcasts or presentations.
Select staff, team members, or performers.
Determine technical requirements of productions or projects.
Manage operations of artistic or entertainment departments or organizations.
Audition or interview potential performers or staff members.
Analyze information obtained from news sources.
Operate still or video cameras or related equipment.
Design layout of art or product exhibits, displays, or promotional materials.
Conduct research to inform art, designs, or other work.
Select resources needed to accomplish tasks.
Set up still or video cameras or related equipment.
Discuss production content and progress with others.
Stay informed about current developments in field of specialization.
Maintain records, documents, or other files.
Confer with clients to determine needs.
Review production information to determine costume or makeup requirements.
Correspond with customers to answer questions or resolve complaints.
Monitor availability of equipment or supplies.
Test performance of electrical, electronic, mechanical, or integrated systems or equipment.
Obtain copyrights or other legal permissions.
Monitor broadcasting operations to ensure proper functioning.
Develop artistic or design concepts for decoration, exhibition, or commercial purposes.
Develop proposals for current or prospective customers.
Present work to clients for approval.
Collaborate with others to develop or refine designs.
Teach classes in area of specialization.
Teach humanities courses at the college level.
Support the professional development of others.
Maintain inventories of materials, equipment, or products.
Enforce rules or policies governing student behavior.
Estimate time or monetary resources needed to complete projects.
Administer tests to assess educational needs or progress.
Evaluate quality of materials or products.
Develop strategies or programs for students with special needs.
Design costumes or cosmetic effects for characters.
Maintain current knowledge related to work activities.
Attend training sessions or professional meetings to develop or maintain professional knowledge.
Arrange items for use or display.
Set up classroom materials or equipment.
Modify teaching methods or materials to accommodate student needs.
Write articles, books or other original materials in area of expertise.
Discuss problems or issues with supervisors.
Collaborate with other teaching professionals to develop educational programs.
Collaborate with others to determine production details.
Teach others to use technology or equipment.
Serve on institutional or departmental committees.
Manage budgets for personal services operations.
Assign duties or work schedules to employees.
Discuss student progress with parents or guardians.
Assign resources or facilities to patrons or employees.
Distribute resources to patrons or employees.
Collect fares or payment from customers.
Order instructional or library materials or equipment.
Inspect equipment to ensure safety or proper functioning.
Disassemble equipment for maintenance or repair.
Install electrical components, equipment, or systems.
Set up material handling gear or equipment, such as rigging, packaging, or temporary structures.
Program equipment to perform production tasks.
Collaborate with others to determine design specifications or details.

emerging_tasks.task: Coordinate the use of drone technology for aerial filming and photography.
Create patterns for costumes based on designer's drawings.
